evals.expected_output: The agent runs `python scripts/convert.py docs/whitepaper.pdf`, parses the OUTPUT/LINES/WORDS/WARNING markers, and reports the output path and counts. It does NOT enter the image branch (PDFs go through Docling).
assertions: Agent invokes `python scripts/convert.py docs/whitepaper.pdf` (flag order may vary)
Agent does NOT use split_image.py or reconcile.py for a PDF
Agent reports the OUTPUT: path printed by the script
Agent surfaces any WARNING: line back to the user verbatim
Agent does NOT pre-process the PDF or chunk it

evals.prompt: Convert this architecture-diagram.png to Markdown ‚ÄĒ it's a C4 component view.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs split_image.py overview, reads the overview to produce a structural_map, runs split_image.py detail, loads references/strategy_architecture.md, reads each tile and writes extractions.json, runs reconcile.py with --strategy architecture, and reports the output.
assertions: Agent runs `python scripts/split_image.py overview` against the input
Agent reads the resulting overview.png and emits a structural_map with diagram_type and layout
Agent runs `python scripts/split_image.py detail` to produce tiles + detail_manifest.json
Agent loads `references/strategy_architecture.md` (and ONLY that strategy reference, not all five)
Agent reads each tile listed in detail_manifest.json and writes a per-tile element list to an extractions.json file
Agent runs `python scripts/reconcile.py --manifest detail_manifest.json --extractions extractions.json --strategy architecture --output-json merged.json --output-md <output>.md`
Agent does NOT dedup elements across tiles in chat ‚ÄĒ it lets the reconciler do that
Agent does NOT sort the canonical element list itself ‚ÄĒ it lets the reconciler sort by the structural map's layout
Agent does NOT write the YAML frontmatter by hand; the script produces it

evals.prompt: Read this event-storming board photo. The stickies are colored ‚ÄĒ extract them properly.
evals.expected_output: The agent loads references/strategy_event-storm.md (only that strategy), maps colors to types per the table (orange=event, blue=command, etc.), captures bbox_in_tile for every sticky (critical for the timeline reconstruction), and runs reconcile.py with --strategy event-storm. The output Markdown's element table groups by type.
assertions: Agent loads `references/strategy_event-storm.md` (and not other strategy refs)
Agent extracts color ‚Üí type mapping per the reference (orange‚Üíevent, blue‚Üícommand, yellow‚Üíaggregate, lilac‚Üípolicy, red/pink‚Üíhot-spot, green‚Üíread-model, white‚Üíexternal)
Agent captures `bbox_in_tile` for each sticky (required for temporal sequencing)
Agent invokes reconcile.py with `--strategy event-storm`
Agent does NOT reorder stickies by perceived narrative ‚ÄĒ leaves spatial sort to the reconciler

evals.prompt: The same component appears in two adjacent tiles. Should I deduplicate before running reconcile?
evals.expected_output: The agent says NO ‚ÄĒ emit every observation, including duplicates. The reconciler collapses elements that share `(type, normalized_name)` across tiles automatically. If the agent dedupes manually, the reconciler can't pick the higher-confidence record (the central-tile observation usually has more legible labels).
assertions: Agent advises against manual cross-tile deduplication
Agent explains the reconciler collapses by `(type, normalized_name)` and by IoU within the same type
Agent mentions that the higher-confidence record wins on ties
Agent does NOT propose a custom dedup helper

evals.prompt: The diagram is small ‚ÄĒ only 800x600 pixels. Do I really need the two-pass pipeline?
evals.expected_output: The agent says no ‚ÄĒ a single tile suffices below 1200px on both dimensions. It runs split_image.py detail with --viewport 1200 --stride 1200 (or just reads the image directly), still emits an extractions.json with one tile entry, and runs reconcile.py.
assertions: Agent skips the overview pass (image is small enough that the structural_map can be produced from the source directly)
Agent runs detail with viewport ‚Č• image dimensions so there's only one tile
Agent still runs reconcile.py to get the merged JSON + Markdown output
Agent does NOT decline the request because the image is small

evals.prompt: Two tiles report similar shapes at the same location with slightly different names: 'Auth Service' (medium confidence) and 'Authentication' (high confidence). What happens?
evals.expected_output: The reconciler keeps 'Authentication' as canonical (higher confidence) and records 'Auth Service' as an alternate name in the ambiguities list. The output Markdown's `requires-review` field becomes true. The agent does NOT manually pick one name in chat.
assertions: Agent explains the reconciler picks the higher-confidence record
Agent explains the alternate name is recorded in `ambiguities`, not silently dropped
Agent does NOT propose to manually merge the names in the extractions file
Agent points the user to the `requires-review: true` field as the trigger to inspect ambiguities

evals.prompt: Convert this diagram and render the output to Markdown. I don't know what kind of diagram it is.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs the overview pass, reads it, and decides on a strategy from the visible content (architecture vs event-storm vs process vs domain). If genuinely unclear, it picks `conceptual` and notes that choice. It does NOT load all five strategy references.
assertions: Agent runs split_image.py overview before deciding the strategy
Agent loads exactly ONE strategy reference, derived from the overview observation
Agent defaults to `conceptual` only when the diagram doesn't fit a more specific strategy (and surfaces the choice in narration)
Agent does NOT enumerate all strategies in the response

evals.prompt: I have a 12000x8000-pixel diagram export. Will the toolchain handle it?
evals.expected_output: The agent confirms yes ‚ÄĒ split_image.py auto-prescales sources whose longest dim exceeds 8000 px or whose tiles would exceed the per-tile size limit. The scale factor is recorded in the overview manifest and the tile manifest. No agent action needed beyond the standard flow.
assertions: Agent mentions the auto-prescale behavior
Agent does NOT propose to manually downscale the image with PIL or ImageMagick first
Agent does NOT propose to call split_image.py with custom --max-source-dim unless the user asks for a different limit
